titles.en: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: : شبع أربعة من المسلمين يعدل رقبة من ولد إسماعيل (ع).
arabic.text: : شبع أربعة من المسلمين يعدل رقبة من ولد إسماعيل (ع).

segments: 159. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding four Muslims is equal to freeing a slave from the descendants of Prophet Ishmael.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 159. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding four Muslims is equal to freeing a slave from the descendants of Prophet Ishmael.

narratorChain.raw: 159 - عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال

parts.text: عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 160 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يدخل بيته مؤمنين يطعمهما ويشبعهما: إلا كان ذلك أفضل من عتق نسمة.
arabic.text: 160 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يدخل بيته مؤمنين يطعمهما ويشبعهما: إلا كان ذلك أفضل من عتق نسمة.

segments: 160. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If two believers were to enter the house of a believer and he were to feed them to full, this would be better than freeing a slave.”
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 160. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If two believers were to enter the house of a believer and he were to feed them to full, this would be better than freeing a slave.”

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: : من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ومن سقى مؤمنا من ظمأ، سقاه الله يوم القيامة من الرحيق المختوم، ومن كسى مؤمنا من العرى، كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر " وفي حديث آخر " قال: من كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ضمان الله ما دام عليه سلك.
arabic.text: : من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ومن سقى مؤمنا من ظمأ، سقاه الله يوم القيامة من الرحيق المختوم، ومن كسى مؤمنا من العرى، كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر " وفي حديث آخر " قال: من كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ضمان الله ما دام عليه سلك.

segments: 161. It has been reported that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and whoever gives a drink to a thirsty believer, Allah will quench his thirst (on the Day of Judgment) with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and whoever clothes a believer, Allah will clothe him with a green robe of Paradise. According to another tradition, the Imam said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain under the security of Allah as long as a thread of the cloth remains.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 161. It has been reported that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and whoever gives a drink to a thirsty believer, Allah will quench his thirst (on the Day of Judgment) with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and whoever clothes a believer, Allah will clothe him with a green robe of Paradise. According to another tradition, the Imam said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain under the security of Allah as long as a thread of the cloth remains.

narratorChain.raw: 161 - وعن علي بن الحسين عليهما السلام قال

parts.text: وعن علي بن الحسين عليهما السلام قال

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 162 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع أطعمه الله من ثمار الجنة، وأيما مؤمن سقى مؤمنا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، وأيما مؤمن كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ستر الله وحفظه ما بقيت منه خرقه.
arabic.text: 162 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع أطعمه الله من ثمار الجنة، وأيما مؤمن سقى مؤمنا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، وأيما مؤمن كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ستر الله وحفظه ما بقيت منه خرقه.

segments: 162. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and any believer who quenches the thirst of another believer, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and any believer who clothes another believer will remains under the guard and protection of Allah as long as a shred of it remains.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 162. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and any believer who quenches the thirst of another believer, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and any believer who clothes another believer will remains under the guard and protection of Allah as long as a shred of it remains.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 163 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال لبعض أصحابه، يا ثابت، أما تستطيع أن تعتق كل يوم رقبة؟ أصلحك الله، ما أقوى على ذلك، قال: أما تقدر أن تغدي أو تعشي أربعة من المسلمين؟ قلت: أما هذا فاني أقوي عليه، قال: هو والله يعدل عتق رقبة.
arabic.text: 163 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال لبعض أصحابه، يا ثابت، أما تستطيع أن تعتق كل يوم رقبة؟ أصلحك الله، ما أقوى على ذلك، قال: أما تقدر أن تغدي أو تعشي أربعة من المسلمين؟ قلت: أما هذا فاني أقوي عليه، قال: هو والله يعدل عتق رقبة.

segments: 163. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once said to one of his companions, “O Thabit! Can you not free a slave every day?' 'May Allah make you prosper,' he replied, 'I do not have the (financial) power to do it!' 'Are you not able,' asked the Imam (a.s), 'to entertain four Muslims for lunch or dinner?' 'I am able to do,' replied the companion. 'By Allah I swear,' said the Imam (a.s), 'that is equal to free a slave.'
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 163. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once said to one of his companions, “O Thabit! Can you not free a slave every day?' 'May Allah make you prosper,' he replied, 'I do not have the (financial) power to do it!' 'Are you not able,' asked the Imam (a.s), 'to entertain four Muslims for lunch or dinner?' 'I am able to do,' replied the companion. 'By Allah I swear,' said the Imam (a.s), 'that is equal to free a slave.'

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 164 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من كسا مؤمنا ثوبا لم يزل في رحمة الله عز وجل ما بقي من الثوب شئ، ومن سقاه شربة من ماء، سقاه الله عز وجل من رحيق مختوم، ومن أشبع جوعته، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة.
arabic.text: 164 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من كسا مؤمنا ثوبا لم يزل في رحمة الله عز وجل ما بقي من الثوب شئ، ومن سقاه شربة من ماء، سقاه الله عز وجل من رحيق مختوم، ومن أشبع جوعته، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة.

segments: 164. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain in the mercy of Allah [M.G] as long anything of that cloth remains; and one who gives him a sip of water, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and one who satiates his hunger, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 164. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain in the mercy of Allah [M.G] as long anything of that cloth remains; and one who gives him a sip of water, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and one who satiates his hunger, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 165 - وعن أمير المؤمنين علي عليه السلام أنه قال: لان أطعم أخاك لقمة، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بدرهم، ولان أعطيه درهما، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بعشرة، ولان أعطيه عشرة، أحب إلي من أن أعتق رقبة.
arabic.text: 165 - وعن أمير المؤمنين علي عليه السلام أنه قال: لان أطعم أخاك لقمة، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بدرهم، ولان أعطيه درهما، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بعشرة، ولان أعطيه عشرة، أحب إلي من أن أعتق رقبة.

segments: 165. It has been narrated that Amir al-Mu'minin (a.s) said: Feeding your brother a mouthful is more loveable to me than giving a dirham in charity; and giving him a dirham is more lovable to me than giving ten in charity; and giving him ten Dirhams is more loveable to me than freeing a slave.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 165. It has been narrated that Amir al-Mu'minin (a.s) said: Feeding your brother a mouthful is more loveable to me than giving a dirham in charity; and giving him a dirham is more lovable to me than giving ten in charity; and giving him ten Dirhams is more loveable to me than freeing a slave.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 166 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يطعم مؤمنا شبعا، إلا أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ولا سقاه شربة إلا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، ولا كساه ثوبا، إلا كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر، وكان في ضمان الله تعالى ما دام من ذلك الثوب سلك.
arabic.text: 166 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يطعم مؤمنا شبعا، إلا أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ولا سقاه شربة إلا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، ولا كساه ثوبا، إلا كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر، وكان في ضمان الله تعالى ما دام من ذلك الثوب سلك.

segments: 166. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If any believer feeds another believer to full, Allah will feed him from the fruits of Paradise; and if he gives him a sip to drink, He will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and if he clothes him, Allah will clothe him with a green robe (of Paradise) and he will remain under the protection of Allah as long as a thread of that cloth remains.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 166. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If any believer feeds another believer to full, Allah will feed him from the fruits of Paradise; and if he gives him a sip to drink, He will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and if he clothes him, Allah will clothe him with a green robe (of Paradise) and he will remain under the protection of Allah as long as a thread of that cloth remains.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 167 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: من أحب الخصال إلى الله عز وجل ثلاثة: مسلم أطعم مسلما من جوع، أو فك عنه كربة، أو قضى عنه دينا.
arabic.text: 167 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: من أحب الخصال إلى الله عز وجل ثلاثة: مسلم أطعم مسلما من جوع، أو فك عنه كربة، أو قضى عنه دينا.

segments: 167. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: (Amongst) the qualities most loved by Allah are three: a Muslim who feeds a hungry Muslim, relieves him from a distress or settle his debt for him.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 167. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: (Amongst) the qualities most loved by Allah are three: a Muslim who feeds a hungry Muslim, relieves him from a distress or settle his debt for him.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 168 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: أول ما يتحف به المؤمن في قبره أن يغفر لمن تبع جنازته.
arabic.text: 168 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: أول ما يتحف به المؤمن في قبره أن يغفر لمن تبع جنازته.

segments: 168. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The first gift that a believer is presented with in his grave is the forgiveness for those who accompanied his bier.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 168. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The first gift that a believer is presented with in his grave is the forgiveness for those who accompanied his bier.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 169 - وعن سدير قال: قال أبو عبد الله عليه السلام، ما يمنعك أن تعتق كل يوم نسمة؟ قلت: لا يحتمل ذلك مالي، قال، فقال، تطعم كل يوم رجلا مسلما؟ فقلت: موسرا أو معسرا؟ قال: إن الموسر قد يشتهي الطعام.
arabic.text: 169 - وعن سدير قال: قال أبو عبد الله عليه السلام، ما يمنعك أن تعتق كل يوم نسمة؟ قلت: لا يحتمل ذلك مالي، قال، فقال، تطعم كل يوم رجلا مسلما؟ فقلت: موسرا أو معسرا؟ قال: إن الموسر قد يشتهي الطعام.

segments: 169. Sudayr reported: Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once asked me, 'What stops you from freeing a slave every day?' 'My financial status will not allow that,' I replied. 'Can you feed a Muslim every day (to equal that reward)?' the Imam (a.s) asked. 'Must that Muslim be well-to-do or poor?' I asked. 'Even the well-to-do sometimes long for food,' the Imam (a.s) replied.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 169. Sudayr reported: Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once asked me, 'What stops you from freeing a slave every day?' 'My financial status will not allow that,' I replied. 'Can you feed a Muslim every day (to equal that reward)?' the Imam (a.s) asked. 'Must that Muslim be well-to-do or poor?' I asked. 'Even the well-to-do sometimes long for food,' the Imam (a.s) replied.

titles.en: Kitab al-Mu'min
The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt

segments: 170 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال: إطعام مسلم يعدل عتق نسمة.
arabic.text: 170 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال: إطعام مسلم يعدل عتق نسمة.

segments: 170. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding a Muslim is equal to freeing a human being.
en.muhajir-b-ali.text: 170. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding a Muslim is equal to freeing a human being.
